description: A designed ICE program-promotion poster in portrait format with a dark, dramatic, desaturated aesthetic. The right side shows a moody black-and-white/blue-tinted photograph of a young Black man wearing eyeglasses and a telephone headset, viewed in profile as if working at a command/dispatch center; faint blue binary-code digits (0s and 1s) are overlaid across the dark background to suggest data and technology. Top-left carries the full-color U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal beside the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' in white serif. Below it, white serif text reads 'Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners' with 'around the world.' emphasized in blue. The lower-left features the large stacked all-caps title in white and blue: 'LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER' with red triple-slash accents and the URL 'ICE.gov/LESC'. The visual style is sleek and authoritative (high contrast, tech/data motif, bold typographic title block). Messaging technique: portrays ICE's Law Enforcement Support Center as a high-tech intelligence hub aiding police worldwide, projecting competence and reach; tied to tweet copy promoting the LESC hotline (802-872-6020) for identifying 'criminal aliens.' Target audience: law-enforcement partners and the public.
notable_text: Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners around the world; LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER; ICE.gov/LESC
